description_long: This is a tall, narrow furniture store building with four stories and a steeply pitched roof featuring dormer windows. The facade is characterized by a traditional half-timbered design, with visible wooden beams and light-colored infill panels. The ground floor includes large windows and a double-door entrance, suggesting a commercial storefront, while the upper floors have regularly spaced rectangular windows. The side elevations are plain, with the timber framing continuing but no windows or decorative features. The roof is dark gray and shingled, with two dormers on the front and one on the rear, adding to the building's historic European appearance. The overall structure is solid and symmetrical, with a clear distinction between the commercial ground floor and the residential or storage upper floors.
description: A tall, narrow, half-timbered furniture store with a steep roof, dormer windows, and a prominent ground-floor storefront. The facade features traditional wooden beams and large display windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with double doors, large display windows, and two dormer windows on the roof.
description_view_1: Side view with plain gable wall, no windows, and visible timber framing pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single door, regular windows, and one dormer window on the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with plain gable wall and timber framing.
attribute_display_windows: Two large rectangular display windows on the ground floor, flanking the main entrance.
